page: Google Cloud Platform m2-megamem-416 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for large-scale in-memory workloads. It is powered by dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processors operating at 2.7 GHz, featuring 416 virtual CPUs mapped to 208 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 5888.0 GB (5.75 TB) of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.15 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data highlights a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics are relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%, the massive core count drives top-tier multi-core performance. The instance achieves top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and Passmark CPU extended instructions. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, with read-only small block cached bandwidth exceeding 20,000 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth reaching 684.81 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text generation for small LLM models show poor performance, indicating potential bottlenecks in specific sequential or instruction-bound tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m2-megamem-416 represents a high-resource density profile where the premium is justified by the massive memory capacity and parallel processing capabilities rather than single-thread speed. This makes it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and heavy data-warehousing workloads. It is also well-suited for parallelized scientific simulations and memory-intensive data processing pipelines, though it is less optimal for workloads dependent on high single-threaded throughput or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform m2-megamem-416 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 416 vCPUs (208 physical cores) powered by 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon processors and 5888.0 GB of RAM. It contains no local storage or GPUs. Designed for massive parallel processing and memory-heavy workloads, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, exceeding 20,000 GB/sec for cached reads. However, single-core performance is weak, falling in the bottom 25% of benchmarks. This trade-off makes the instance highly efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and complex data-warehousing tasks that leverage its massive memory footprint, while remaining less suitable for single-threaded applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-megamem-416 is a memory-optimized server featuring 416 vCPUs, 5.75 TB of RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors, designed for massive in-memory databases and parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-megamem-416 is a memory-optimized server with 416 vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM, built for large-scale in-memory workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive parallel processing power and extensive RAM capacity for enterprise in-memory databases.
bullet_points: Equipped with 416 vCPUs and 5.75 TB of dedicated system memory.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERPs.
Lacks local storage and integrated GPU hardware.
Exhibits lower single-core efficiency compared to multi-core throughput.
